what_works: Monochrome base with subtle colour pops, serif/sans pairing for drama, design-tool aesthetic (dashed borders, corner handles), extreme whitespace, 96px hero heading, playful interactive details, minimap sidebar

elements.hero: Massive typography, centred, no image needed
elements.content: Card-based with dashed selection borders and corner dots
elements.colour: Grey-scale with one accent used very sparingly
elements.typography: Display serif for headings, geometric sans for body, tight tracking on large text
elements.interaction: Drag gestures, hover reveals with backdrop blur, scroll-triggered content

what_works: Instrument Serif for headings, 12-column CSS Grid with breakout regions for full-width moments, fluid clamp() typography, dark/light theme toggle, accordion animations with cubic-bezier easing, full-width hero slider with manual navigation
best_for: Lifestyle brands, boutique retail, premium small businesses

elements.nav: Minimal top bar with dark/light variant, hamburger menu
elements.hero: Full-width image slider with editorial serif overlay text
elements.content: Split-grid layouts alternating image and text, breakout regions
elements.colour: Restrained palette with dark and light theme states
elements.typography: Instrument Serif headings, system sans body, fluid sizing via clamp()
elements.interaction: Smooth accordion expand/collapse (400ms cubic-bezier), hover effects on footer links

what_works: Numbered portfolio sections (01, 02, 03) create editorial structure, minimalist dark aesthetic, capitalized bold tagline as hero, clean information hierarchy, prefetch optimisation for snappy navigation
best_for: Creative agencies, production companies, service businesses with strong portfolio

elements.nav: Toggle menu with minimal chrome, high contrast
elements.hero: Typography-led hero with capitalised statement, no hero image needed
elements.content: Numbered portfolio grid with thumbnail carousel, hierarchical sections
elements.colour: Dark base with high contrast text, minimal accent
elements.typography: Bold sans-serif for statements, clean hierarchy through size and weight
elements.interaction: Prefetch on link hover for instant navigation, back-to-top anchor

what_works: Official Next.js View Transitions demo showing shared element morphing, Suspense reveals, directional navigation slides, and same-route crossfades -- all using React ViewTransition component with no animation libraries
best_for: Technical reference for implementing page transitions in Next.js 16.2+

elements.nav: Anchored header that stays fixed during page transitions
elements.hero: Photo grid with thumbnail-to-hero morph animation
elements.content: Gallery grid with photographer tabs using crossfade transitions
elements.colour: Clean neutral palette focused on showcasing the transition effects
elements.interaction: Shared element morph, directional slide (forward/back), skeleton-to-content reveal, tab crossfade

what_works: Comprehensive collection of CSS scroll-driven animation demos using animation-timeline: scroll() and view(). All examples are CSS-only with no JavaScript, showcasing progress bars, parallax, reveal animations, and sticky headers
best_for: Technical reference for replacing AOS.js/GSAP ScrollTrigger with native CSS scroll animations

elements.typography: Monospace for code, clean sans for labels
elements.interaction: Every demo is scroll-driven using only CSS animation-timeline property

what_works: Cultural storytelling as web design — warm beige palette, Cheltenham BT serif throughout, poetic hero text that reads like a love letter to Bombay, recipe sharing section, B-Corp badge, 25M meals donated counter. Proves a restaurant site can be a cultural platform, not just a reservation tool
best_for: Restaurants, cafes, food businesses, any brand with a heritage story to tell

elements.nav: Clean horizontal with Book a Table as primary CTA, location dropdown
elements.hero: Poetic text-led hero with warm-toned food/culture photography
elements.content: Sectioned by experience type (cafes, menus, shop, stay), recipes as content marketing
elements.colour: Warm beige backgrounds, black text, golden yellow accents for announcements
elements.typography: Cheltenham BT serif throughout for classic premium feel
elements.interaction: Location-based menu switching, newsletter signup with cultural storytelling

what_works: Warm colour sectioning (cream → taupe → dark plum) creates visual rhythm without cards or borders. Playfair Display + Source Sans Pro pairing balances elegance with readability. Coral CTAs (#fd5f58) pop against neutral backgrounds. Process transparency section and 6-month warranty build trust. Houzz badge + Dulux/Farrow & Ball partnerships add authority
best_for: Painters, decorators, interior trades, any home-service business wanting to feel premium

elements.nav: Sticky header with phone number and prominent GET A QUOTE button
elements.hero: Full-width banner with centred text overlay, key selling propositions (time, standard, budget)
elements.content: Alternating background-colour sections, service cards with consistent icon treatment
elements.colour: Coral #fd5f58 CTAs, cream #efebe7, taupe #ded9d4, dark plum #442e3a
elements.typography: Playfair Display serif for headings, Source Sans Pro for body — sophisticated contrast
elements.interaction: Multiple conversion paths (phone, WhatsApp, email, form), 2-hour response promise

what_works: Anti-corporate accountancy brand that proves boring industries can have bold design. Vibrant purple (#6a0dad) with turquoise accents, illustrated character hero ('accounting for you beautiful weirdos'), custom Castledown font, animated gradient text. BBC/Sky/Forbes press logos. Quote builder completes in under 60 seconds
best_for: Accountants, financial services, any professional service wanting to stand out from corporate competitors

elements.nav: Clean with prominent Get a Quote CTA in navigation bar
elements.hero: Illustrated character in quirky armour with bold anti-corporate tagline
elements.content: Service cards by business type, testimonials with named clients, award badges
elements.colour: Vibrant purple #6a0dad primary, turquoise #08ffc4 accent, white backgrounds
elements.typography: Custom Castledown font for headlines, animated gradient text effects
elements.interaction: 60-second quote builder, animated badge reveals, gradient text animations

what_works: Radical simplicity — the entire site is essentially three location cards, a menu, and a founder story. Proves a restaurant only needs location, menu, and narrative. Oswald + Roboto Condensed typography. No booking system (walk-in only) keeps it honest. Founder story ('Tim and Jordan's dream') adds soul
best_for: Small indie restaurants, cafes, takeaways — any food business that doesn't need complexity

elements.nav: Minimal horizontal: three locations + vouchers + careers
elements.hero: Three large food photography cards doubling as location navigation
elements.content: Structured menu display with clear pricing, founder narrative section
elements.colour: Neutral palette — dark text on light backgrounds, photography provides all colour
elements.typography: Oswald bold for headings, Roboto Condensed for body — contemporary and clean
elements.interaction: Location cards as primary navigation, social media (Instagram) integration
